growth_records.agit: 850 rpm (NH4Cl membrane bioreactor); 800 rpm (urea bioreactor); batch shaker 100 motions/min

up.units: mmol/day (bulk reactor, 2 L membrane bioreactor working volume)

growth_records.cite: Picone et al. Ammonia oxidation at pH 2.5 by a new gammaproteobacterial ammonia-oxidizing bacterium. The ISME Journal (2020). DOI:10.1038/s41396-020-00840-7. [PMC8115276]
growth_records.snippet: KHCO3 consumption indeed increased exponentially over time... we calculated the growth rate at pH 3.5 to reach 0.196 day-1, corresponding to a doubling time of 3.5 days.
growth_records.notes: Growth rate mu=0.196 day-1 (doubling time 3.5 days) explicitly reported by authors, calculated from exponential increase in KHCO3 consumption (proxy for acid production during NH4+ oxidation) during initial 20-day batch phase of a 2 L continuous-flow membrane bioreactor fed 76.2 mmol NH4Cl/day. Converted to per-h: 0.196/24=0.008167 h-1; doubling time 3.5*24=84 h. Uptake rate: NH4+ consumed = fed (76.2 mmol/day) - not consumed (17.7 mmol/day) = 58.5 mmol/day (Table 1 nitrogen balance, chemostat/continuous-mode operation after switch from batch); NOT normalized per gDW (no biomass mass given), so reported as bulk reactor rate only, low-medium confidence. Secretion rates for NO2-, NO3-, NO/NO2 gas taken directly from Table 1 (values reported as mean +/- SD, n=3), also bulk reactor rates not gDW-normalized.

growth_records.agit: 850 rpm (NH4Cl membrane bioreactor); 800 rpm (urea bioreactor); batch shaker 100 motions/min
growth_records.medium: AOB medium with 50 mM urea instead of NH4Cl, double trace elements, pH 3.5

up.units: mmol/day (bulk reactor, 1.5 L membrane bioreactor working volume, over 8 h exponential batch period)

growth_records.cite: Picone et al. Ammonia oxidation at pH 2.5 by a new gammaproteobacterial ammonia-oxidizing bacterium. The ISME Journal (2020). DOI:10.1038/s41396-020-00840-7. [PMC8115276]
growth_records.snippet: growth rate (mu) with urea was calculated from NO3- formation, which followed an exponential curve (y = 0.711e^0.1038x, R2 = 0.997) and indicated a mu = 0.104 day-1 and a doubling time of 6.7 days.
growth_records.notes: Growth rate mu=0.104 day-1 (doubling time 6.7 days) calculated by authors from exponential fit of NO3- formation curve (y=0.711e^0.1038x, R2=0.997; exponent 0.1038 day-1 ~ reported mu 0.104 day-1) during cultivation of a 1.5 L membrane bioreactor fed 31.1 mmol urea/day. Converted to per-h: 0.104/24=0.004333 h-1; doubling time 6.7*24=160.8 h. Uptake rate: urea consumed = fed (31.1 mmol/day) - not consumed (27.7 mmol/day) = 3.4 mmol/day, measured over an 8 h period in the exponential phase of the batch reactor (Table 1); not gDW-normalized, bulk reactor rate only. Secretion rates for NO2-, NO3-, NO/NO2 gas taken directly from Table 1 (n=3).
